content: read reviews/1159.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1159.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1159.md.

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist â€” `deepmind/1159.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdإ‘s Problem 1159`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1159`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (blocking sets in finite projective planes with bounded multiplicity).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[ESS83]` and `[Va99]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1159 ... end Erdos1159`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1159`).
- [x] `open Configuration Classical Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 37).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A â€” no `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1159` (snake_case matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 51]`.
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[Va99, 4.70]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Does there exist an absolute constant C > 1 such that â€¦" â€” this is a **yes/no question** â†’ **Case 1**.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) â†” P`.
- [x] Problem is open, so `answer(sorry)` is correct (not `answer(True)` or `answer(False)`).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: existence of `C : â„•` with `1 < C` and the blocking-set property for all finite projective planes.
- [x] Quantifiers (`âˆƒ C`, `âˆ€ (P L : Type)`, `âˆ€ l : L`) appear **after** `answer(sorry) â†”`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` â€” a valid category.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body, consistent with `research open`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 51`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 51).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order** (5 < 51).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = combinatorics, 51 = geometry).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` followed by `sorry` on the next line (multi-line form, acceptable per guidelines).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`/`abbrev`/`structure` (N/A â€” none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A â€” no variants declared.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`âˆ€`, `âˆƒ`, `âˆˆ`, `â‰¤`, `â†”`, `â„•`, `âˆ§`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` â€” not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass â€” not verified in this review.

---

**Verdict:** All checkable items pass. The formalization correctly captures the yes/no question about bounded blocking sets in finite projective planes using the `answer(sorry) â†” P` pattern. The use of `C : â„•` (rather than `â„‌`) is appropriate since an integer ceiling of any real bound suffices. The typeclass setup `[Membership P L] [Fintype P] [Fintype L] [Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L]` correctly abstracts over all finite projective planes. Pending build verification.

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdإ‘s Problem 1159

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1159](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1159)

Determine whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that every finite projective plane
has a blocking set where no line is hit more than $C$ times. A blocking set is a set of
points that meets every line.

Erdإ‘s, Silverman, and Stein [ESS83] proved this is true with
$|S \cap \ell| \ll \log n$ for all lines $\ell$ (where $n$ is the order of the projective
plane).

[ESS83] Erdإ‘s, P., Silverman, R., and Stein, A.

[Va99] Vardy, A.
-/

open Configuration Classical Finset

namespace Erdos1159

/--
Erdإ‘s Problem 1159 [Va99, 4.70]:

Does there exist an absolute constant $C > 1$ such that every finite projective plane
has a set of points $S$ meeting every line in at least $1$ and at most $C$ points?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 51]
theorem erdos_1159 : answer(sorry) â†”
    âˆƒ C : â„•, 1 < C âˆ§
      âˆ€ (P L : Type) [Membership P L] [Fintype P] [Fintype L]
        [Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L],
        âˆƒ S : Finset P,
          âˆ€ l : L, 1 â‰¤ (S.filter (fun p => p âˆˆ l)).card âˆ§
                    (S.filter (fun p => p âˆˆ l)).card â‰¤ C := by
  sorry

end Erdos1159

content.text: The review passed. The verdict says "All checkable items pass" â€” the only unchecked boxes are build verification, which is explicitly noted as not verified in the review (and not something to fix in the `.lean` file). No changes needed.
